name: DeployKit
shortDescription: DeployKit - أداة تحديث دلتا لتطبيقات سطح المكتب .NET
fullDescription: خادم توزيع تحديثات ذاتي الاستضافة لتطبيقات سطح المكتب. يقوم كل مطور بنشر نسخته الخاصة - لا استضافة مركزية، تحكم كامل.
features: مُستضاف ذاتيًا - انشر على خادمك الخاص (Docker، Render، VPS)
لوحة تحكم - واجهة مستخدم ويب لتسجيل التطبيقات، وإنشاء مفاتيح API، وتحميل التحديثات
واجهة برمجة تطبيقات التحديث - نقاط نهاية عامة لتطبيقات العميل للتحقق من التحديثات واسترجاعها
آمن - كلمات مرور BCrypt، مصادقة رمز Bearer، تحديد معدل الطلبات، رؤوس CSP، تسجيل التدقيق، قفل الحساب
خفيف الوزن - قاعدة بيانات SQLite، حاوية Docker واحدة (حوالي 200 ميجابايت)
متعدد المنصات - يعمل على Windows وLinux وmacOS
حزمة تطوير البرامج NuGet - تكامل بسطر واحد لتطبيقات سطح المكتب WPF

name: Wifi-Keys
shortDescription: تطبيق MAUI لنظام Android بتقنية .NET لاستعادة كلمات مرور شبكات الواي فاي باستخدام خوارزمية استبدال الأحرف fh_/wlan. يدعم اللغات العربية والإنجليزية والفرنسية. متوفر بنمطين: داكن وفاتح.
fullDescription:
تطبيق Android لاكتشاف شبكات WiFi القريبة واستخراج كلمات المرور باستخدام خوارزمية تحويل الأحرف المخصصة.

ملاحظة:
 هذا التطبيق مخصص فقط للشبكات التي تبدأ بـ `fh_` أو `wlan` — وهي شبكات مشفرة بخوارزمية معروفة.
features: مسح شبكات WiFi القريبة
استخراج كلمات المرور للشبكات المدعومة (fh_ / wlan)
واجهة عربية / إنجليزية / فرنسية
وضع النهار والليل مع كشف تلقائي من النظام
اتصال مباشر بالشبكة
نسخ كلمة المرور

name: Web-OS
shortDescription: Web OS — a complete desktop operating system that runs in your browser AND as a native desktop app via Electron. Kernel, VFS, window manager, terminal with real shell, 17 apps — zero framework dependencies.
fullDescription:
# Web OS

**A complete desktop operating system — runs in your browser AND as a native desktop app via Electron.**

Kernel, persistent file system, window manager, terminal with a real shell,
process scheduler, permission system, background services, and 17 applications
— built entirely from scratch in TypeScript with **zero runtime dependencies**.

---

## Quick Start

```sh
npm install
npm run dev          # Browser mode → http://localhost:5173
npm run electron:dev # Electron desktop mode (native windows, tray, shortcuts)
```

Production build:
```sh
npm run build        # Static site → dist/
npm run electron:start # Production Electron (run build first)
npm run build:electron # Package installer (NSIS/AppImage/deb)
```

---

## Architecture

```
Apps (17 applications)
  │
  ├─ system.* API  ←  Permission Layer  ←  PermissionManager
  │
  ├─ Service Layer   (FileIndex, AutoSave, NetworkWatch, GlobalSearch)
  │
  ├─ Kernel          (ProcessManager, ProcessScheduler, SystemEventBus)
  │
  ├─ VFS + IndexedDB (persistent virtual file system)
  │
  └─ System Control Layer (SCL) — Electron bridge
       │
       └─ Electron Main Process
            │
            ├─ Native FS (real filesystem via Node.js)
            ├─ Hardware API (CPU, RAM, battery, displays)
            ├─ Network API (real interfaces)
            ├─ Clipboard (OS-level)
            ├─ Native Dialogs (open/save/folder)
            ├─ System Tray
            ├─ Global Shortcuts (Win+E, Win+I, Win+R, etc.)
            └─ Auto Updater
```

### Dual-Platform Design

| Feature | Browser Mode | Electron Desktop Mode |
|---------|-------------|----------------------|
| **Storage** | IndexedDB (VFS) | VFS + real filesystem (Drives C:, D:, etc.) |
| **Display** | Fixed size | Native resolution/refresh rate control |
| **Filesystem** | Virtual only | Full real OS access (Desktop, Documents, Downloads, etc.) |
| **Hardware** | Mock/simulated | Real CPU, RAM, battery, display info |
| **Network** | Simulated | Real network interfaces and status |
| **Clipboard** | Browser API | OS-level clipboard (text + image) |
| **Dialogs** | Browser prompt() | Native file pickers |
| **Power** | N/A | Shutdown, restart, sleep |
| **System Tray** | N/A | Tray icon with context menu |
| **Shortcuts** | N/A | Global hotkeys |

All 17 apps work identically in both modes. The SCL (System Control Layer) auto-detects Electron and delegates to native APIs.

---

## Applications

| App | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| **Terminal (OrcaTerm/osh)** | Real shell: pipes `|`, redirection `` ``, ``, quoting, `~`/`$VAR` expansion, tab completion, persistent history, ~40 commands (`ls`, `grep`, `tree`, `ps`, `kill`, `neofetch`, `cowsay`, `js`, `curl`, …) |
| **Files** | Grid/list views, breadcrumbs, cut/copy/paste, drag--drop, rubber band selection, rename/delete/properties, import/export, drive detection |
| **Scribe** | Text editor with line numbers, dirty tracking, Ctrl+S, Markdown preview |
| **Paint** | Pen/eraser/shapes/flood-fill/eyedropper, undo/redo, PNG export |
| **Calculator** | Hand-written expression parser (no `eval`), history tape |
| **Viewer** | Image viewer with zoom, set as wallpaper |
| **Music Player** | Audio player, playlist management, shuffle, repeat |
| **Video Player** | Video playback within the VFS |
| **Keys** | Two-octave WebAudio synth piano with demo song |
| **System Monitor** | Process table, End-task, CPU/heap charts, FPS |
| **Task Manager** | Detailed process management with search/filter |
| **Settings** | Themes (dark/light), accent colors, wallpapers, real display resolution/refresh rate, storage, factory reset |
| **Clipboard** | Clipboard history manager with pin/unpin |
| **Trash** | Restore or permanently delete trashed files |
| **Network** | Connection status, IP config, real interface info |
| **Screenshot** | Capture full screen, region, or active window |
| **Welcome** | First-boot tour |

---

## Features

### Desktop
- Window manager with dragging, 8-direction resizing, edge snap, minimize/maximize/close
- Taskbar with pinned apps, running processes, system tray, clock
- Start menu with app launcher and file search
- Desktop icons with rubber band selection, right-click context menus
- Lock screen, boot sequence, shutdown screen
- Multiple wallpapers, dark/light themes, accent colors

### Filesystem
- Full virtual file system persisted to IndexedDB (survives browser reloads)
- In Electron mode: real OS drives (C:, D:, removable USB), Desktop/Documents/Downloads folders
- Automatic USB drive detection and mounting (Windows)
- Import/export files to/from the real computer
- Trash with restore and permanent delete

### Terminal (osh)
- 40+ built-in commands
- Pipe chains, redirection, command chaining
- Tab completion, persistent history (up/down arrows)
- Environment variables, home directory `~`
- `js` command for inline JavaScript evaluation
- `neofetch`, `cowsay`, `tree`, `curl`, `grep`

### Native Desktop Features (E
features: Window manager with dragging, 8-direction resizing, edge snap, minimize/maximize/close
Taskbar with pinned apps, running processes, system tray, clock
Start menu with app launcher and file search
Desktop icons with rubber band selection, right-click context menus
Lock screen, boot sequence, shutdown screen
Multiple wallpapers, dark/light themes, accent colors

Import/export files to/from the real computer

Environment variables, home directory ~

name: OrcaOS
shortDescription: 64-bit x86-64 educational kernel with Ring 3 user mode, ELF64 loader, OrcaFS, shell, and cross-platform build (MSYS2 + Linux)
fullDescription:
# OrcaOS

An educational **64-bit x86-64** kernel written in C with assembly glue. OrcaOS boots via QEMU PVH (or GRUB Multiboot2), manages memory, files, processes, and runs ELF64 **user-mode programs** at Ring 3 with full hardware isolation via `iretq`.

---

## Architecture

```
                          ┌──────────────────────┐
                          │    User Process       │ Ring 3
                          │  (ELF64, int 0x80)    │
                          └──────────┬───────────┘
                                     │ syscall
                          ┌──────────▼───────────┐
                          │  interrupt 0x80       │ Ring 0
                          │  IDT → isr_stubs.S   │
                          │  → syscall.c handler  │
                          └──────────┬───────────┘
                ┌────────────────────┼────────────────────┐
                │                    │                    │
         ┌──────▼──────┐    ┌───────▼───────┐    ┌───────▼───────┐
         │  Scheduler   │    │  VFS + OrcaFS  │    │   Memory      │
         │  round-robin │    │  block/ATA     │    │  PMM/Paging   │
         └──────────────┘    └───────────────┘    └───────────────┘
```

| Layer | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Boot** | `boot.asm` — Multiboot2 header, enters 64-bit long mode, identity-maps first 1 GB with 2 MB pages |
| **PMM** | Physical Memory Manager — bitmap-based, parses Multiboot2 memory map, Next-Fit allocation |
| **Paging** | 4-level page tables (PML4→PDPT→PD→PT), `map_page`/`unmap_page`, 2 MB↔4 KB split, USER flag propagation |
| **Heap** | Kernel heap at `0x100000000` — free-list allocator with splitting, forward coalescing, dynamic expansion (`kmalloc`/`kfree`) |
| **GDT** | 7 entries: null, kernel code/data, user code/data (Ring 3), TSS with RSP0 |
| **IDT** | 256 entries: 32 exception ISRs, 16 IRQs (PIC-remapped to 32–47), `int 0x80` syscall (DPL 3) |
| **Syscall** | `SYS_WRITE` (print to VGA), `SYS_READ` (stub), `SYS_EXIT` (return to Ring 0) |
| **Process** | Static table (64 processes), states: UNUSED/READY/RUNNING/BLOCKED/EXITED, user-mode `process_create_user()` |
| **Scheduler** | Round-robin queue (64 entries), `scheduler_next()` skips EXITED/UNUSED |
| **Shell** | Interactive CLI (`orca` prompt), 11 commands, AZERTY keyboard, path-aware |
| **VFS** | In-memory directory tree backed by OrcaFS with persistence across reboots |
| **OrcaFS** | Custom flat filesystem: superblock (sector 1), inode table (sectors 2–99), data blocks (sector 100+) |
| **ATA** | PIO 28‑bit LBA driver — polled mode, single/multi-sector read/write |
| **VGA** | Text mode 80×25, hardware cursor, COM1 serial mirror |

---

## Project Structure

```
OrcaOS/
├── bootloader/
│   └── boot.asm                    # Multiboot2 + 64-bit entry
├── kernel/
│   ├── kernel.c                    # Boot sequence, built-in ELF test
│   ├── kernel.h
│   ├── idt.c / idt.h               # Interrupt descriptor table
│   ├── isr_stubs.S                 # ISR/IRQ/syscall stubs (cross-platform GAS)
│   ├── syscall.c / syscall.h       # System call handler
│   ├── pvh_note.s                  # Xen PVH note section
│   ├── editor.c / editor.h         # Simple text editor
│   ├── ports.h                     # I/O port helpers
│   ├── types.h                     # Custom types
│   ├── memory/
│   │   ├── pmm.c / pmm.h           # Physical memory manager
│   │   ├── paging.c / paging.h     # 4-level page tables
│   │   ├── heap.c / heap.h         # Kernel heap
│   │   └── mem_util.h              # num_to_string helper
│   ├── cpu/
│   │   ├── gdt.c / gdt.h           # GDT setup
│   │   ├── gdt_reload.S            # lgdt (cross-platform)
│   │   ├── tss.c / tss.h           # Task State Segment
│   │   └── ltr_test.s              # LTR instruction helper
│   ├── process/
│   │   ├── process.c / process.h   # Process manager
│   │   ├── scheduler.c / .h        # Round-robin scheduler
│   │   └── context_switch.S        # switch_to_user (iretq)
│   ├── elf/
│   │   ├── elf.c / elf.h           # ELF64 loader
│   ├── fs/
│   │   ├── vfs.c / vfs.h           # Virtual file system
│   │   ├── file.c / file.h         # File descriptor API
│   │   ├── block.c / block.h       # Block device abstraction
│   │   ├── path.c / path.h         # Path resolver
│   │   └── orcafs/
│   │       ├── orcafs.c / orcafs.h # OrcaFS mount/file ops
│   │       └── inode.c / inode.h   # Inode table + directory entries
│   └── shell/
│       ├── shell.c / shell.h       # Interactive command shell
├── drivers/
│   ├── vga.c / vga.h               # VGA text-mode driver
│   ├── keyboard.c / keyboard.h     # PS/2 AZERTY keyboard
│   └── disk/
│       └── ata.c / ata.h           # ATA PIO driver
├── gen_elf.py                      # Minimal ELF64 binary generator
├── user_test.asm                   # NASM user-mode test program
├── user_test.lds                   # Linker script for user programs
├── linker.ld                       # Kernel linker script
